notes: Nicola's loop on the floor. The verbs are hostess work: greet at the door, seat the party, pour water, smooth the apron. Plus interior verbs that emerge when Aria activates (LISTEN INSIDE, ANSWER, NAME ARIA). Engine: HARVEST is the spendable currency (her quiet attention); BLOOM (engine var _bloom) is the positive doom-clock — the night holding. Doubt and stagnation are negative tracks.

cards.title: GREET THE PARTY
cards.flavor: At the maître d' stand. Welcome them with the smile that is not, strictly, what the smile means. Take the coats. Ask after the wife / husband / child who they referenced last visit and you remembered.

cards.title: SEAT AT THE TABLE
cards.flavor: Walk a party to their table. Three steps ahead, the menu tucked under your arm. The infuriating oblivious grace is the gait. You are very good at this. You have stopped, this year, being able to tell whether that is a compliment.

cards.title: SMOOTH THE APRON
cards.flavor: The small unconscious gesture that has, since Tuesday, also been a hand on the flat-becoming-not-flat stomach. The room does not, today, notice. Dante noticed last week. He did not, today, mention it.

cards.title: POUR WATER FOR THE TABLE
cards.flavor: The pitcher from the bar. Walk back to the table. Pour without splashing. Refill without being asked. The small ritual the floor runs on.

cards.title: LISTEN INSIDE
cards.flavor: Stop on the floor for a beat. Listen for the second voice that has, in the past week, started talking. It says: who am I. It says: where is my father. It says, today, this is not a body, this is a protocol. You do not, today, answer it. You listen.

cards.title: NAME ARIA
cards.flavor: Stand at the maître d' stand. Take the breath. Say the name in your head: Aria. The second voice, hearing the name, becomes the named thing — quieter, more articulate, a little less afraid. The flag aria_named_by_nicola goes on. Once.

cards.title: WALK TO TABLE 14
cards.flavor: Cross the dining room. The booth in the far corner where the out-of-town suit has been sitting for two hours without ordering. Take the order. Or, more likely, take the small folded note he is going to leave weighted under a hundred-dollar bill.

cards.title: INTERCOM TO THE HELM
cards.flavor: Sammy lets you press the intercom button behind the bar. The line connects to the helm. You say: Mr. D, the gentleman at fourteen. He says: I see him. You say: thank you. You hang up. The brass speaker clicks.

cards.title: TAKE THE BACK STAIR
cards.flavor: Down to the lower deck. The catering office is on your left; the card room is further in. You have, until tonight, taken this stair twice. Dante is sending you down with something. You do not, this time, ask what.

cards.title: THE INFURIATING GRACE
cards.flavor: The small smile that means something other than the words. The gait that does not seem to register the weight you are, in fact, carrying. The room reads you as effortless because you have, by year three, taught the room to. Once per night.

cards.title: CHECK THE RESERVATION BOOK
cards.flavor: The book at the stand. Pencil with the worn-down eraser. You run your finger down the column. You match what is on the page to what is on the floor. You write the small mark in the margin that means seated.

cards.title: WALK THE FLOOR
cards.flavor: From the stand through the dining room and back. Three steps ahead of yourself, the way you have learned. You see the table that needs the water topped, the table that has finished and not been cleared, the table that is about to wave. You log it. You walk back.

cards.title: STAND WITH GLORIA
cards.flavor: She has, twice in three years, set the water glass on the corner of the stand without looking at you. Tonight is the third. You take it. You drink half. You stand beside her at the pass for one beat the way she would. The not-saying is the saying.

cards.title: CLEAR WITH TOMÁS
cards.flavor: He bumps a bus tub against his right hip the way Sammy taught him. You hand him the stacked plates the way Sammy taught you. The two of you turn over a four-top in under ninety seconds without speaking. He says, on the way past: she ate the second course too. He keeps walking.

cards.title: INTERCOM ALBERTO
cards.flavor: Sammy lets you press the second intercom button. The line goes to whichever phone Alberto is at. You say: Mr. Alberto. He says: nicola. He waits. You say the small specific thing. He says: I'll be by. He hangs up. Sammy does not, today, look up at you from the well.

cards.title: ANSWER ANTONIO
cards.flavor: The stand line. You see his name on the small display. You pick up. You say: nic? He says: nic. You answer the first question. He decides whether to ask the second. He decides, again, not.

cards.title: WALK PAST TABLE 17
cards.flavor: The corner booth. You take the long way around the dining room to pass it. The booth is empty tonight; Paul comes Sundays. The leaded window is dark with the muddy divide. You feel, in passing, the place where the wall thins.

cards.title: SIGN THE MANIFEST
cards.flavor: Cole from Halsey Farms at the back door with the figs and the pomegranates and the half-flat of late blackberries Hector did not order. You sign. You write the time (10:18). You initial. You hand back the manifest copy. You write the pickup down in the small private accounting.

cards.title: PASS THE BACK ROOM
cards.flavor: The door without the sign. By invitation only. You walk past it on your way to the locker room. The door is closed. It has been closed for forty minutes. Somebody is winning. You do not, this Friday, stop walking.

cards.title: TAKE THE BREATH
cards.flavor: The small private breath at the stand between seatings. Inhale through the nose for the count of four. Hold for the count of two. Out through the mouth for six. The third, inside you, settles. Aria, when she is active, listens.
